title: Puerto Banus Boat Rental Snorkeling Guide 2026
html:
Snorkeling in Puerto Banus is best accessed by boat, with rentals from EUR 300 per half-day from the main marina. BoatHire24 connects you with verified boats for exploring the Costa del Sol's clear waters. Whether you want a private motor yacht or a budget-friendly speedboat, you can compare options online and secure your spot.

Why rent a boat for snorkeling in Puerto Banus?

Renting a boat gives you access to remote coves and clearer water than the busy beaches. The coastline between Marbella and Estepona has several Marine Protected Areas where fish and marine life thrive. With your own vessel, you can anchor off Cabopino or the cliffs of Sierra Bermeja and enjoy a secluded swim. Many boats include snorkel masks and fins as standard equipment.

Where to set off in Puerto Banus

Most boat rentals depart from Puerto Banus Marina, a purpose-built harbour with easy boarding and parking. Nearby options include the Real Club Náutico de Marbella, the fishing port of La Bajadilla, and the smaller marina at Estepona. For those based further west, Sotogrande Marina is another starting point. The Bay of Marbella and the coves around Cabo Pino offer calm waters ideal for snorkeling. The islet of Piedra del Hombre is a local hotspot for underwater life, about four nautical miles south-west of the port.

Which boat to rent

Choose a boat that suits your group size and budget, with a focus on easy swimming access. Many vessels come with ladders and a platform for getting in and out of the water.

  1. Axopar 37, a fast, open dayboat for up to 12 guests with a large swim platform; from EUR 800 half-day.
  2. Olympic 490, a reliable speedboat for four to six people, ideal for short trips; from EUR 350 half-day.
  3. Azimut 38, a compact flybridge with a stern boarding ladder, suitable for two to five guests; from EUR 600 half-day.
  4. Mefasa 90, a crewed motor yacht with a swim platform and flybridge for up to twelve people; from EUR 1,500 half-day. For details on this model, see the Mefasa 90 listing.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Puerto Banus?

Prices range from EUR 100 per person for a shared tour to EUR 2,000 for a crewed yacht for a full day. Budget options include shared snorkeling tours from EUR 120 for a three-hour trip. The exact cost, however, can vary by season, boat size, and duration. For a private small motorboat, expect to pay from EUR 300 for a half-day (four hours). Catamarans such as a Lagoon 42 cost from EUR 1,000 for a full day. Crewed luxury motor yachts like a Sunseeker 60 are typically EUR 2,000, 3,500 per day. You can check the latest prices for boat rental Puerto Banus offers on BoatHire24.

  • Shared snorkeling tour: EUR 100, 150 per person, 3 hours.
  • Private small motorboat (half-day): EUR 300, 600 for up to 6 guests.
  • Catamaran (full day): EUR 800, 1,500 for up to 12 guests.
  • Crewed luxury yacht (full day): EUR 2,000, 5,000 for up to 12 guests.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Puerto Banus?

Spain generally requires a licence for powerboats over a certain length, but many rentals are provided with a skipper. If you have a valid International Certificate of Competence (ICC) or a recognised national licence, you may be able to bareboat charter a small vessel. However, regulations can change, and we recommend that operators confirm exact requirements.

In summer (July to August), the Poniente wind can strengthen in the afternoon, so plan your snorkeling anchorages for the morning when the sea is calm and visibility is highest.
Many visitors opt for a skippered charter, which removes any licensing concerns and allows you to relax and enjoy the trip.

At a glance

  • Price range: EUR 120, 3,500 per day, depending on boat type and season.
  • Peak months: June, July, August.
  • Trip length: 3 hours (shared) to full day (8 hours).
  • Capacity: 6, 12 guests, but larger yachts can accommodate up to 20.
  • Currency: EUR (euros) accepted everywhere.

Questions about boat rental in Puerto Banus

Can I bring my own snorkel gear?

Yes, most operators allow you to bring your own equipment, though many boats provide goggles, fins, and snorkels as standard.

Is it safe to snorkel from a boat without a skipper?

Only if you are a confident swimmer and check local wind forecasts, as the sea state can change quickly in summer afternoons.

What marine life might I see?

Common sightings include bream, octopus, and occasionally small rays, especially around the rocky reefs near Cabopino.

Do I need to reserve in advance?

Yes, especially during July and August when boats are booked weeks ahead, so early booking ensures you secure your preferred vessel.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Puerto Banus and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
